title: dysoxylum alliarum
intro: Dysoxylum alliarum, also known as East Indian rosewood, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, with its heartwood valued for its aromatic and medicinal properties. The plant's heartwood contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Dysoxylum alliarum has been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. It can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment for its medicinal and health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
